wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the tg-vibecoders-dashboard project. Explain its purpose as a lightweight analytics dashboard for Telegram chat activity over 24-hour and 7-day windows. Describe the ultra-MVP design philosophy: minimal dependencies, no migrations, single-server architecture with Next.js. Document the core value proposition—real-time KPIs, trend analysis, and LLM-powered digest generation without complex setup. Highlight key architectural decisions such as server-side data fetching via API routes, PostgreSQL integration for message storage, and OpenAI Responses API for structured output. Include practical examples from the codebase like the Mermaid diagram showing data flow from UI to DB to LLM and back. Explain how this system enables non-technical users to gain insights from chat data through visualizations (Chart.js) and AI summaries. Address common use cases including community moderation, engagement tracking, and content discovery. Provide context on deployment targets like Railway/Vercel/Render and emphasize simplicity in operations. Make this accessible to beginners while giving experienced developers insight into scalability trade-offs and technical constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: app/ Directory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the `app/` directory of the tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Explain its role as the core of the Next.js App Router architecture, housing pages, API routes, components, hooks, and utilities. Detail how `page.tsx` files define routes (e.g., `/` for 24-hour view, `/week` for 7-day view) and render UI via `DashboardShell`. Describe the function of `layout.tsx` in providing shared UI structure. Break down subdirectories: `api/` for backend endpoints like `/api/overview` which fetch analytics data; `components/` organized into atomic design patterns (atoms, charts, tables, filters); `hooks/` for reusable logic such as formatting; and `utils/` for time-related helpers. Use concrete examples from `DashboardShell.tsx` to show how client-server interaction occurs through state management and API calls. Clarify file naming conventions (`route.ts` for API handlers, `page.tsx` for UI pages) and their routing implications. Address how separation of concerns enhances maintainability and scalability.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the backend API endpoints in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Document each endpoint's HTTP method, URL pattern, request parameters, response schema, and error codes. For GET /api/overview, detail query parameters like 'days' and 'chat_id', the PostgreSQL-backed aggregation logic, and the comprehensive JSON response containing KPIs, charts, and entity rankings. For GET /api/report/generate, explain the date-based digest generation flow, OpenAI integration via Responses API, Zod validation enforcement using DailyDigestSchema, and error handling for timeouts or schema mismatches. For GET /api/report/insights, describe how LLM-powered insights are generated from message previews with polling-based retrieval. For GET /api/report/preview, document its role in preparing structured data for LLM processing. Include real code snippets showing SQL queries, parameter parsing, and response formatting. Address authentication assumptions, rate limiting implications, and environment-dependent behaviors.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the atomic UI components used in the tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Explain the purpose and implementation of `KpiCard.tsx` as a reusable metric display unit, `KpiRow.tsx` as a container that composes multiple KPI cards into a structured layout, and `SummaryList.tsx` for rendering bullet-point summaries from LLM-generated insights. Document component props, expected data types (e.g., `kpi` object structure), and integration with parent `DashboardShell`. Include usage examples showing how `KpiRow` receives aggregated metrics from API responses. Address accessibility features such as semantic HTML and ARIA labels if present. Discuss styling via Tailwind CSS classes and responsiveness across screen sizes. Provide guidance on extending these components for new KPI types or summary formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Time Window Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the time window analytics feature that enables users to analyze Telegram chat activity over two distinct periods: 24-hour and 7-day views. Explain how the `/` (default) and `/week` routes serve as entry points for different temporal scopes, triggering appropriate data fetching from the backend. Detail the implementation of `WindowSelect` component which allows interactive switching between time windows and propagates the selected value to API calls via state management. Describe how time-based filtering impacts all downstream KPIs, charts, and tables by altering the dataset boundaries. Use actual logic from page routing and filter controls to demonstrate real-time reactivity. Include examples of URL patterns, state synchronization between UI and data layer, and performance implications of larger time windows. Address common issues such as timezone handling using `useTimeFormatting` and edge cases when insufficient data exists for a selected period.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prompt Construction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the prompt construction process in the LLM integration pipeline. Explain how buildDigestUserPrompt uses structured data from buildDailyPreview to create a well-formed input for the OpenAI Responses API. Document the format of the injected JSON payload, including date context, time window, and message data. Illustrate with code examples how user prompts are constructed with clear instructions for JSON-only output and strict schema adherence. Describe the role of SYSTEM_PROMPT in guiding model behavior and ensuring consistent formatting. Address best practices for prompt engineering in this context, such as clarity, precision, and avoiding hallucinations by restricting source data. Include troubleshooting tips for malformed prompts and localization considerations given the Russian-language comments in the code.

wiki_catalogs.name: KPI Metrics Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the KPI metrics processing module that calculates core analytics including total messages, unique users, reply rates, and link sharing statistics. Explain how the PostgreSQL queries aggregate data across configurable time windows using COUNT and DISTINCT operations with conditional filtering. Detail the implementation of average messages per user calculation and peak hour detection from hourly trend data. Include examples from the codebase showing parameterized queries with dynamic WHERE clauses for chat filtering and date range selection. Document the normalization of results into consistent numeric formats and the handling of edge cases like zero users. Address performance considerations such as index usage on sent_at and chat_id columns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overview API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for GET /api/overview endpoint in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Document that it accepts query parameters 'days' (integer, default 1, range 1-30) and 'chat_id' (string, optional). Explain how the endpoint connects to PostgreSQL via pg pool, executes time-windowed aggregations based on the 'days' parameter, and returns a comprehensive JSON response containing KPIs (total_msgs, unique_users, avg_per_user, replies, with_links), hourly/daily message trends, top entities (users, links, words, threads), specialized analysis (unanswered questions, top helpers, error patterns, artifacts), and social metrics (hashtags, mentions, forwarded content). Include code snippets showing SQL queries for message volume, user engagement, thread analysis, and error detection. Detail the response schema structure with examples. Address error handling for missing DATABASE_URL and internal server errors. Explain integration with frontend components like DashboardShell and KpiRow. Provide usage examples for fetching 24-hour and 7-day analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hourly Trend Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the hourly trend analysis implementation that processes message activity in hourly buckets. Explain how the PostgreSQL date_trunc('hour', sent_at) function groups timestamps into hourly intervals and how generate_series ensures complete 24-point coverage even with sparse data. Describe the client-side handling of ISO-formatted timestamps and the zero-filling logic that maintains consistent time series structure. Include practical examples from both /api/overview and buildDailyPreview showing UTC alignment, window boundary handling, and peak hour calculation. Address challenges in visualizing low-volume periods and strategies for maintaining temporal continuity in chart rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Volume Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the message volume metrics calculation that includes total messages and hourly/daily trends. Explain how the PostgreSQL queries use COUNT(*) with date_trunc functions to aggregate message counts across time windows. Detail the implementation of the generate_series function for ensuring complete hourly data points and handling gaps in activity. Include examples from the codebase showing the base WHERE clause construction for time and chat filtering. Document the normalization of timestamps to ISO format and the edge case handling when no messages exist. Address performance considerations such as index usage on sent_at and chat_id columns, and how query results are mapped to frontend chart components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Links Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the TopLinksTable UI component. Explain its purpose in displaying the most frequently shared URLs within the Telegram chat analytics dashboard. Document the component's props interface (TopLinksTableProps) and how it receives an array of link objects with 'url' and 'cnt' properties. Describe the rendering logic, including conditional null return when no data is present, URL formatting with target='_blank', and integration of useNumberFormatter for count display. Highlight its responsive layout via 'panel' and 'max-h-64' classes, and its placement within the DashboardShell grid using 'lg:col-span' utilities. Provide a code example showing mock data usage. Address accessibility considerations for external links and potential enhancements such as domain grouping or click tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Users Ranking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the top users ranking feature that calculates user engagement based on message count and reply interactions. Explain how SQL queries aggregate messages per user with JOINs against participant tables and use ORDER BY clause to rank by volume. Detail the inclusion of reply metrics through self-joins on message threads and how KPIs are derived from these rankings. Include code examples showing SELECT statements with COUNT() and GROUP BY, as well as response formatting in the API layer. Address edge cases such as inactive users, tie-breaking logic, and performance optimization using database indexes on user_id and timestamp columns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unanswered Questions Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the unanswered questions analysis feature that identifies root messages containing question-like patterns without replies. Explain the heuristic rules using regex and keyword matching (e.g., presence of '?' or terms like 'как', 'почему', 'ошибк', 'не работает') to detect potential questions. Describe how the system uses a NOT EXISTS subquery to verify absence of replies across the dataset, ensuring only truly unanswered messages are captured. Detail the post-processing logic that calculates message age in hours and filters results to include only those older than 12 hours. Include performance considerations such as query chunking and indexing strategies on message_id and reply_to_message fields. Provide examples from the codebase showing how preview snippets are truncated and formatted for display in the UI.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the technology stack of tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Detail each framework and library used: Next.js 15.4.6 for App Router routing and server components, React 19.1.1 for component-based UI rendering, pg 8.16.3 for PostgreSQL connectivity, openai 5.12.2 for LLM interactions using the Responses API, chart.js 4.5.0 for time-series visualizations, and zod 3.23.8 for runtime validation of LLM outputs. Explain version constraints (Node.js >=18) and development tooling including TypeScript 5.9.2, Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling, PostCSS for processing, and dotenv for environment configuration. Discuss why these technologies were chosen—e.g., Next.js for hybrid static/dynamic rendering, Zod for schema safety with AI-generated JSON. Include real code examples such as how `zod` validates the `DailyDigest` schema in `digest_schema.ts`. Cover performance implications, developer experience benefits, and compatibility considerations across the stack. Address debugging tools, hot reloading via `next dev`, and production optimization through `next build/start`.

wiki_catalogs.name: lib/ Directory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the `lib/` directory of the tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Explain its purpose as a modular business logic layer decoupled from presentation and routing concerns. Detail two main subdirectories: `llm/` containing OpenAI integration code (`report.ts` for LLM requests, `shared.ts` for common types and utilities) and `report/` housing report generation logic including schema definitions (`schema.ts`, `digest_schema.ts`), data slicing (`slice.ts`), and rendering functions (`digest_render.ts`). Highlight how `index.ts` re-exports these modules for clean imports. Emphasize the use of Zod for runtime validation of LLM outputs via `digest_schema.ts`. Illustrate integration points with API routes (e.g., `/api/report/generate`) and frontend components. Provide examples showing how typed data flows from database to LLM to UI. Discuss benefits of abstraction for testability and reuse across services.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Integration Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the LLM integration pipeline in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Focus on how generateReportFromPreview orchestrates OpenAI Responses API calls using strict JSON schema validation (DailyDigestJsonSchemaForLLM) to ensure predictable outputs. Detail the end-to-end flow: from building a preview via buildDailyPreview, constructing prompts using buildDigestUserPrompt, executing time-limited API requests with timeout safeguards, parsing responses, validating against Zod schemas, and rendering final markdown via renderDigest. Explain error handling strategies for common failure modes (timeout, empty content, invalid JSON, schema mismatch). Highlight security aspects such as environment-controlled API keys and model selection. Use actual code excerpts to illustrate retry logic, streaming considerations, and validation workflows. This should enable developers to debug, extend, or replicate the LLM interaction pattern safely.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chart Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the charting system in tg-vibecoders-dashboard, focusing on `DailyChart.tsx` and `HourlyChart.tsx`. Describe how each component visualizes temporal message activity using Chart.js, with `HourlyChart` displaying 24-hour trends and `DailyChart` showing 7-day patterns. Detail the data input structures (`hourlyRows`, `dailyRows`) derived from `/api/overview` and how timestamps are formatted using `useTimeFormatting` hook. Explain conditional rendering logic in `DashboardShell` based on the selected time window. Document configuration options for datasets, color schemes, tooltips, and responsive canvas behavior. Include code examples demonstrating data transformation from API response to chart labels and data points. Address performance considerations like re-renders with `useMemo` if implemented, and provide troubleshooting tips for common rendering issues such as empty datasets or incorrect time zones.

wiki_catalogs.name: KPI Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Key Performance Indicator (KPI) system that displays core engagement metrics including total messages, unique users, average messages per user, reply rates, and link-sharing volume. Explain the structure and reusability of the `KpiCard` component, which renders individual metrics with support for custom formatting via prop functions or default number formatting through `useNumberFormatter`. Detail how `KpiRow` composes multiple `KpiCard` instances into a responsive grid layout, adapting column count based on screen size. Document the data flow from the `/api/overview` endpoint to the frontend, highlighting schema alignment between backend response and KPI props. Provide code examples showing how numeric values are transformed (e.g., rounding averages to two decimal places). Discuss accessibility considerations like semantic HTML and ARIA IDs for testing. Address customization options for styling and formatting, and troubleshooting tips for missing or NaN values due to data anomalies.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Execution and Timeout Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the API execution layer in the LLM integration pipeline. Detail how generateReportFromPreview initializes the OpenAI client using environment-controlled credentials (OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL) and constructs requests with model-specific parameters like max_output_tokens. Explain the timeout mechanism using Promise.race to enforce hard deadlines (default 120s) and prevent hanging requests. Use actual code excerpts to demonstrate error handling for 'openai_timeout' scenarios and request cancellation logic. Discuss configuration options via REPORT_TIMEOUT_MS and REPORT_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS environment variables. Provide guidance on tuning these values based on performance requirements and cost considerations. Include debugging strategies for failed API calls and monitoring techniques for tracking request latency and success rates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Temporal Trend Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the temporal trend analysis system that generates hourly and daily activity patterns. Explain how the date_trunc function in PostgreSQL extracts time buckets and how generate_series ensures complete hourly coverage even with sparse data. Describe the client-side mapping of timestamps to ISO strings and the aggregation logic that maintains chronological order. Include practical examples from both /api/overview and buildDailyPreview implementations showing window alignment to UTC days and proper handling of time boundaries. Address challenges in visualizing irregular message distributions and strategies for smoothing incomplete data points.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for GET /api/report/generate endpoint in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Document that it requires 'date' parameter in YYYY-MM-DD format and optionally 'chat_id', 'since', and 'until' for custom time windows. Explain the authentication requirement of OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable. Detail the workflow: building message preview via buildDailyPreview, sending context to OpenAI's Responses API using generateReportFromPreview, enforcing Zod validation through DailyDigestSchema, and returning structured LLM output. Include code examples showing request construction, OpenAI integration with proper error handling for timeouts (AbortError) and validation failures (422). Explain the retry logic and request ID propagation for debugging. Describe the expected JSON response structure with digest sections like summary, highlights, recommendations. Address rate limiting considerations and caching strategies for expensive LLM calls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Daily Trend Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the daily trend analysis implementation that aggregates message activity across calendar days. Explain how the PostgreSQL date_trunc('day', sent_at) function partitions data into daily intervals and how the system handles partial days at window boundaries. Describe the UTC-based day alignment and the mapping of date-truncated timestamps to ISO strings for frontend consumption. Include practical examples from /api/overview showing multi-day window processing and proper grouping semantics. Address challenges in comparing uneven day lengths and strategies for presenting longitudinal trends across variable time windows.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Engagement Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the user engagement metrics module that calculates unique users, average messages per user, and top contributors. Explain how DISTINCT user_id counting works in PostgreSQL and how the average per-user message rate is derived. Detail the implementation of username normalization logic that combines first/last names with usernames into human-readable display formats. Include examples from the codebase showing the LEFT JOIN between messages and users tables, and the COALESCE/NULLIF patterns used for safe string handling. Document the top users query with LIMIT 10 and ORDER BY count, and how results are formatted for display. Address performance considerations including indexing on user_id and the impact of large result sets on memory usage during processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Words Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the TopWordsTable UI component. Explain its role in showcasing the most frequently used words in chat messages, aiding in trend identification. Document the TopWordsTableProps interface with 'word' and 'cnt' fields, and describe how the component maps over rows to render each word-frequency pair. Detail the use of useNumberFormatter for consistent numeric presentation and the table's visual styling with header casing and spacing. Explain the component's conditional rendering behavior when empty and its responsive constraints via max-h-64. Illustrate how this table integrates into the overall dashboard layout and provide a practical example with sample word data. Discuss potential improvements like stop-word filtering or stemming support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Links Ranking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the top links ranking feature that identifies frequently shared URLs in chat messages. Explain how regex pattern matching extracts URLs from message text in both preprocessing and post-processing stages. Detail the normalization process including domain extraction, protocol stripping, and duplicate consolidation. Describe how link frequency is aggregated using Map objects in memory and sorted before being truncated for preview display. Include examples from slice.ts demonstrating URL parsing utilities and their integration with the overview route. Address challenges like shortener detection, spam filtering, and handling malformed URLs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Helper Leaderboard Computation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the helper leaderboard computation that ranks users based on their contributions to others' threads. Explain the recursive CTE (Common Table Expression) implementation that traces reply chains to identify the original thread author (root message). Describe how the system distinguishes between self-replies and external contributions by comparing reply_user_id with root_msg.user_id. Detail the aggregation logic that counts valid cross-user replies and limits results to the top 10 contributors. Include insights into performance optimization through indexed queries on reply_to_message and message_id fields. Provide real code examples showing how user display names are normalized using first_name, last_name, and username fields. Address edge cases such as deleted users or missing profile data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Directory Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the directory layout of the tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Break down the three main directories: `app/` containing pages, API routes, and UI components following Next.js App Router conventions; `lib/` housing shared business logic for LLM processing and report generation; and `scripts/` with maintenance utilities for testing and smoke checks. Explain the role of root-level configuration files including `next.config.js` for customizing webpack and runtime, `tsconfig.json` for TypeScript settings, `postcss.config.mjs` for CSS processing with Tailwind, and `.env.example` for environment setup. Use concrete examples such as how `app/api/overview/route.ts` serves analytics data while `lib/llm/report.ts` handles OpenAI calls. Clarify file naming patterns (`page.tsx`, `route.ts`) and their significance in the routing system. Illustrate how separation of concerns improves maintainability—components live in `app/components`, hooks in `app/hooks`, and styles in `globals.css`. This should help new contributors navigate the codebase efficiently.

wiki_catalogs.name: scripts/ Directory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the `scripts/` directory of the tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Describe its role in supporting development workflows through standalone Node.js scripts written in JavaScript. Explain each script's purpose: `smoke-digest.mjs` and `smoke-insights.mjs` for testing LLM-generated digest and insights pipelines; `smoke-openai.mjs` for validating OpenAI API connectivity and response handling; and `test-report.mjs` for end-to-end verification of report generation logic. Detail how these scripts are used during CI/CD or local debugging to ensure reliability of external integrations. Include usage instructions (e.g., environment setup, execution commands). Highlight their importance in catching integration failures early, especially around API rate limits, authentication issues, or schema mismatches. Provide practical examples of output interpretation and troubleshooting steps based on script results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Processing Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the server-side data processing engine that powers analytics in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Explain how the /api/overview route executes complex PostgreSQL queries to aggregate message volume, user engagement, link sharing, top entities, unanswered questions, helper leaderboard, error patterns, artifacts, hashtags, mentions, and forwarded content across configurable time windows. Describe the use of advanced SQL constructs like CTEs and recursive queries for thread analysis. Detail client-side post-processing in Node.js for word extraction, link parsing, and normalization logic (e.g., normalizeUsernameOrId). Cover performance optimizations such as chunked queries for reply mapping and efficient in-memory aggregation using Maps. Include practical examples from the codebase showing hourly/daily trend extraction, top entity computation, and heuristic-based detection of questions or errors. Address scalability concerns and database indexing requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Filter Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the interactive filter components `ChatSelect.tsx` and `WindowSelect.tsx` that control dashboard data scope. Explain how `ChatSelect` enables users to choose between different Telegram chats by rendering a dropdown populated with chat metadata from the API response, triggering refetches when changed. Document `WindowSelect`'s role in toggling between 24-hour and 7-day analytics views, updating the `days` state that drives both data fetching and chart selection. Describe event handling through `onChange` callbacks passed down from `DashboardShell`, and how URL parameters are constructed via `URLSearchParams` during fetch requests. Include examples of state synchronization between filters and the main data loader. Address accessibility aspects like keyboard navigation and screen reader support, and discuss styling consistency using shared Tailwind classes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Temporal Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create in-depth documentation for the temporal analysis module that visualizes message trends over time using interactive charts. Cover the `HourlyChart` component, which renders a line chart displaying message volume per hour across a 24-hour window, leveraging Chart.js for rendering and `build24hRange` utility for filling gaps in sparse data. Explain how timestamps are normalized to UTC and formatted locally using `useTimeFormatting` hooks for consistent display. Similarly, detail the `DailyChart` component that shows daily message counts over a week using a bar chart, with dynamic labels and responsive sizing. Illustrate how both components use `useEffect` and `useMemo` for efficient re-rendering and chart instance lifecycle management. Include examples of data shaping from API responses (`hourlyRows`, `dailyRows`) into chart-ready series and labels. Address performance best practices such as canvas cleanup, memoization, and handling empty datasets gracefully.

wiki_catalogs.name: Response Parsing and Schema Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the response validation phase of the LLM pipeline. Explain the two-step validation process: first parsing raw JSON output from the model, then validating against the Zod schema (DailyDigestSchema). Use code examples to show how invalid JSON is caught with contextual snippets for debugging, and how schema mismatches are reported with precise path and message details. Highlight the difference between DailyDigestJsonSchemaForLLM (used in API request for strict mode) and DailyDigestSchema (used for runtime validation), emphasizing their complementary roles in ensuring type safety. Discuss error recovery strategies and logging practices, including requestId propagation for traceability. Address common issues like missing fields, type mismatches, and unexpected nulls, providing mitigation techniques for developers extending the schema.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Ranking Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the entity ranking engine that identifies top users, links, words, threads, helpers, errors, hashtags, and mentions. Explain how SQL GROUP BY clauses combined with ORDER BY and LIMIT extract ranked lists from raw message data. Detail the in-memory aggregation using Map objects for word frequency counting and link extraction with regex parsing. Cover specialized ranking logic such as recursive CTEs for thread depth analysis and heuristic-based error token detection. Include examples from the codebase showing stopword filtering, case normalization, and preview truncation. Address scalability concerns when processing large message volumes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Insights Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for GET /api/report/insights endpoint in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Document that it accepts the same parameters as /generate: 'date', 'chat_id', 'since', and 'until'. Explain its dependency on OPENAI_API_KEY and similar error handling patterns for missing keys and timeouts. Detail how it differs from /generate by calling generateInsightsFromMessages instead of generateReportFromPreview, producing different analytical outputs focused on behavioral insights rather than daily digests. Include code snippets showing the insight generation pipeline and response formatting. Explain use cases for this endpoint in providing deeper analysis of user interactions and content patterns. Describe the polling mechanism used by clients to retrieve insights asynchronously. Provide guidance on handling partial results and timeout scenarios during prolonged LLM processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interaction Rate Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the interaction rate metrics that track replies, threads, and unanswered questions. Explain how JSON path conditions (raw_message ? 'reply_to_message') are used in PostgreSQL to identify reply messages and thread roots. Detail the recursive CTE implementation in the helpers leaderboard query that traces reply chains back to original threads. Include examples from the codebase showing the question detection heuristics using LIKE patterns and character position checks for question marks. Document the logic for identifying unanswered questions by checking non-existence of replies both within the time window and across the entire database. Address performance considerations around recursive queries and the trade-offs between pre-aggregation and real-time computation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Threads Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the TopThreadsTable UI component. Explain its function in identifying message threads with the highest reply counts, indicating active discussions. Document the component's props structure containing 'root_id', 'replies', and 'root_preview' fields. Describe the three-column layout design and its extended width via 'lg:col-span-2' for better readability. Explain the use of formatNumber for reply counts and the presentation of thread previews. Highlight the component's overflow handling and integration within the dashboard's grid system. Provide a usage example with realistic thread data. Address limitations such as lack of thread navigation and suggest possible enhancements like direct linking or sentiment analysis overlay.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Words Ranking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the top words ranking feature that analyzes message content for keyword frequency. Explain how message text is tokenized, normalized (lowercasing, punctuation removal), and filtered using stopwords lists. Detail the in-memory aggregation using JavaScript Map objects to count word occurrences across large datasets efficiently. Cover the integration between backend processing in route.ts and utility functions in slice.ts for word slicing and frequency sorting. Include examples of stopword filtering, minimum length thresholds, and truncation for UI display. Address performance considerations when processing high-volume chats and potential enhancements like stemming or sentiment tagging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Artifacts Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the artifacts tracking feature that detects development-related content such as GitHub links or code blocks in messages. Explain the domain-specific detection logic using predefined artifact domains (github, vercel, netlify, etc.) matched against extracted URLs. Describe how the presence of triple backticks ('```') is used as an indicator of code snippets. Detail the in-memory processing pipeline that scans message text for these signals and constructs artifact records with previews, URLs, and code indicators. Include guidance on extensibility—how new domains or pattern types can be added to enhance detection coverage. Provide practical examples from the codebase showing how message previews are truncated and structured for UI rendering. Discuss limitations and false positive mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the frontend layer of tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Describe the component hierarchy starting from `layout.tsx` and `page.tsx`, which bootstrap the application, to `DashboardShell.tsx` as the main container orchestrating all UI elements. Explain the atomic design pattern implemented in `app/components/atoms/` (e.g., `KpiCard.tsx`, `SummaryList.tsx`) and reusable chart components (`HourlyChart.tsx`, `DailyChart.tsx`). Detail how client-side interactivity is managed via `DashboardClient.tsx` using React hooks for state and effects. Document data flow from API responses to component props, emphasizing server-to-client hydration. Include usage of React 19 features like actions if applicable. Provide examples of component composition—how `KpiRow.tsx` aggregates multiple `KpiCard` instances. Address styling via `globals.css` and Tailwind classes, responsive behavior, and accessibility practices. Discuss performance optimizations such as lazy loading or memoization if present. This should serve both newcomers learning UI structure and experts extending the interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the error handling strategy employed across backend services in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Analyze how API routes wrap critical operations in try-catch blocks and return standardized error payloads with appropriate HTTP status codes (400, 422, 500, 503, 504). Detail specific error types handled: missing environment variables (DATABASE_URL, OPENAI_API_KEY), invalid input parameters (date format, chat ID), database connectivity issues, OpenAI API timeouts (AbortError), empty or malformed LLM responses, and JSON schema validation failures. Show how request IDs are captured and propagated for debugging. Explain logging practices and error enrichment (e.g., adding request_id to responses). Provide guidance on extending error handling for new endpoints while maintaining consistency. Use real code examples from route handlers and LLM functions to illustrate defensive programming patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Tables
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document all table components in the tg-vibecoders-dashboard that display ranked entities and specialized analysis results. Cover `TopLinksTable`, `TopWordsTable`, `TopThreadsTable`, `UnansweredTable`, `TopErrorsTable`, `TopHelpersTable`, `ArtifactsTable`, `HashtagsTable`, `MentionsTable`, and `ForwardedFromTable`. Explain their uniform design pattern: receiving sorted row data from `ApiData` via props, rendering paginated or limited entries, and presenting key metrics like counts or frequencies. Detail column structures, sorting assumptions (server-side vs client-side), and interaction patterns such as hover effects or click-to-expand details if applicable. Highlight shared dependencies on TypeScript interfaces for row shapes and formatting hooks like `useNumberFormatter`. Provide examples of how each table integrates within `DashboardShell`'s grid layout, including responsive stacking behaviors on smaller screens. Discuss potential extensions like adding search, export functionality, or dynamic column visibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Rankings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the entity ranking system that identifies and displays top-performing elements within the chat ecosystem, including users, links, words, and threads. Explain how each dedicated table component (`TopLinksTable`, `TopWordsTable`, etc.) receives sorted data from the backend and renders it in a consistent, paginated format. Highlight shared patterns such as limit enforcement (top N entries), sorting logic (descending by count), and integration with global filters like chat selection and time window. Use real data structures from API responses to show field mappings (e.g., `user_id`, `link_url`, `word`, `thread_id`). Describe how these tables support quick insights into viral content, influential participants, and trending topics. Include guidance on extending these tables with additional interactivity like sorting headers or tooltips. Address potential issues such as truncated text, special character handling in words, and URL sanitization for security.

wiki_catalogs.name: Markdown Rendering and Output Formatting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the final stage of the LLM pipeline: rendering validated JSON into human-readable Markdown. Explain how renderDigest transforms a DailyDigest object into a compact, Telegram-friendly format with emoji indicators and structured sections (discussions, insights, stats). Use code examples to illustrate string joining logic, conditional inclusion of optional fields like insights, and formatting rules for participant lists and outcomes. Discuss design decisions behind the visual layout, such as prioritizing brevity for chat consumption and using consistent headers. Address extensibility—how developers can customize the template or add new sections—and provide guidelines for maintaining readability across different locales. Include performance considerations given that rendering occurs on every successful digest generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Analysis Modules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for specialized analysis modules including unanswered questions detection, helper leaderboard computation, artifacts tracking, and forwarded content analysis. Explain the heuristic rules for identifying questions (presence of '?' or specific keywords) and determining lack of replies through NOT EXISTS subqueries. Describe the recursive CTE implementation that traces reply chains to identify original thread authors. Detail the domain-specific detection of development artifacts via URL patterns and code block indicators. Cover the complex UNION ALL queries that consolidate forward origins from different Telegram API fields. Include performance optimization techniques like chunked queries for reply mapping.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Preview API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for GET /api/report/preview endpoint in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Document that it serves as a data preparation endpoint for LLM processing, accepting 'date', 'chat_id', 'since', and 'until' parameters. Explain how it validates date format (YYYY-MM-DD), handles chat filtering, and uses buildDailyPreview to extract structured message data for a given time window. Detail its role in the report generation pipeline as input provider for both /generate and /insights endpoints. Include code examples showing the preview data structure with message excerpts, metadata, and contextual information. Explain error responses for missing database connection (missing_database_url) and invalid date parameters. Describe how DEFAULT_CHAT_ID environment variable provides fallback chat selection. Address performance implications of large message sets and pagination considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unanswered Questions Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the UnansweredTable UI component. Explain its critical role in surfacing unanswered questions (older than 12 hours) to improve community responsiveness. Document the useState hook for managing expandable row states and the onClick handler that toggles full message visibility. Describe the props interface with 'id', 'preview', 'hours', and 'text' fields, and explain the dual mapping strategy for main and expanded rows. Detail the accessibility implications of the cursor-pointer and hidden row patterns. Highlight the increased height constraint 'max-h-[360px]' accommodating longer content. Provide an example demonstrating expansion behavior. Discuss performance considerations for large text payloads and suggest improvements like lazy loading or excerpt generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Threads Ranking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the top threads ranking feature that identifies the most active conversation threads. Explain how recursive Common Table Expressions (CTEs) in PostgreSQL traverse message reply chains to calculate thread depth and total replies. Detail the SQL logic that identifies root messages and aggregates child responses, then ranks threads by engagement. Include examples from the overview route showing CTE structure and result mapping to frontend components. Discuss limitations of recursion depth in SQL, performance implications on large datasets, and caching strategies for frequently accessed time windows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Forwarded Content Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the forwarded content analysis that aggregates sources of forwarded messages from both 'forward_from_chat' and 'forward_origin' Telegram API fields. Explain the use of UNION ALL queries to consolidate data from different schema paths into a unified result set. Describe how chat IDs are normalized into short identifiers and used to construct t.me URLs for direct linking. Detail the logic for handling both channel forwards and user forwards separately, including name resolution and URL generation. Cover the dual-query approach that provides both filtered (per selected chat) and global (all chats) forwarding statistics. Include performance notes on query execution and indexing requirements on raw_message JSON fields. Provide examples from the codebase showing how titles, usernames, and message IDs are extracted and formatted for presentation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the backend services in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Focus on the API route system within `app/api/` that exposes endpoints like `/api/overview`, `/api/report/generate`, `/api/report/insights`, and `/api/report/preview`. Explain how these server-side routes query PostgreSQL using the `pg` client, aggregate chat analytics (message volume, user engagement, link sharing), and return structured JSON. Detail the LLM integration pipeline where `generate/route.ts` sends prompts to OpenAI's Responses API with strict schema enforcement via Zod (`digest_schema.ts`). Describe error handling, rate limiting considerations, and database connection management. Use actual code snippets from `route.ts` files to show SQL queries and response formatting. Highlight security aspects such as environment-variable-controlled access to `DATABASE_URL` and optional SSL via `PGSSL`. This should enable developers to understand, debug, and extend API functionality safely.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for the PostgreSQL connection management system used in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Explain how the Pool instance from the 'pg' library is initialized with DATABASE_URL and optional SSL configuration via PGSSL environment variable. Detail connection lifecycle within API routes: acquisition via pool.connect(), usage during query execution, and guaranteed release in finally blocks to prevent leaks. Discuss configuration options such as SSL settings and connection pooling defaults. Highlight security implications of environment-controlled credentials and encrypted connections. Provide best practices for managing long-running queries and avoiding connection exhaustion under load. Include code examples showing proper try-finally patterns and error-safe cleanup.

wiki_catalogs.name: State and Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for client-side state management in the frontend layer. Focus on `DashboardShell.tsx` as the central state orchestrator using React `useState` and `useEffect` hooks to manage `days`, `chatId`, `data`, and `error` states. Explain the data flow lifecycle: user interactions update filter state → `useEffect` triggers API calls to `/api/overview` → response data populates `ApiData` state → downstream components re-render with new props. Detail abort signaling with `AbortController` to prevent race conditions. Document custom hooks `useNumberFormatter.ts` and `useTimeFormatting.ts` as reusable utilities for consistent value presentation across components. Illustrate how formatting logic is abstracted from presentation components to promote consistency. Include diagrams showing unidirectional data flow and component dependency graph. Address error handling strategies, loading states with skeleton UI, and memory efficiency considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Analysis Modules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Provide detailed documentation for the suite of specialized analysis modules designed to surface nuanced insights beyond basic metrics. Cover `UnansweredTable` which identifies questions posed in chat that received no replies, enabling moderation follow-up. Explain `TopErrorsTable` that detects and ranks frequent error messages, aiding debugging and system monitoring. Detail `ForwardedFromTable` for tracking external content sources, revealing information flow origins. Document `HashtagsTable` and `MentionsTable` for social graph and topic tracking. Describe `ArtifactsTable` for identifying generated content (e.g., code blocks, logs), and `TopHelpersTable` for recognizing users who frequently respond to others. For each, explain the backend query logic, frontend presentation, and business value. Use actual filtering and aggregation techniques from the codebase. Include examples of how these tables enhance operational awareness and community management. Address limitations such as detection accuracy and scalability with large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Errors Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the TopErrorsTable UI component. Explain its purpose in tracking frequent error tokens or keywords in developer-focused chats, helping identify recurring technical issues. Document the component's acceptance of error data with 'token' and 'cnt' properties, and its use of formatNumber for count formatting. Describe the minimalist design with uppercase header and compact layout. Explain its placement strategy using 'lg:col-span-1' for proportional grid allocation. Highlight the conditional rendering when no errors are detected. Provide a practical example with common error types. Discuss integration possibilities with external issue trackers and potential enhancements like error categorization or suppression rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Helpers Ranking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the top helpers ranking feature that identifies users who frequently respond to unanswered questions. Explain how the system detects unanswered messages within a time window and tracks subsequent replies to them. Detail the SQL logic that correlates question messages with follow-up responses by different users, then aggregates helper contributions per user. Describe how this data powers the TopHelpersTable component and influences KPI calculations. Include examples from the API route and table component showing data shaping and display logic. Address accuracy concerns such as false positives in question detection and response attribution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core analytical features of the dashboard. Cover time-windowed analytics (24h vs 7d views via `/` and `/week`), chat selection interface, and real-time metric updates. Explain KPI tracking including total messages, unique users, reply rates, and link sharing shown in `KpiCard` and `KpiRow` components. Detail temporal analysis using `HourlyChart` and `DailyChart` for trend visualization. Document entity ranking systems: top users, links, words, threads via dedicated tables. Include specialized modules like `UnansweredTable`, `TopErrorsTable`, `ForwardedFromTable`, and `HashtagsTable` for deep content inspection. Show how filtering via `ChatSelect` and `WindowSelect` controls impacts data display. Use real component logic to demonstrate data binding and interaction patterns. This should allow users to fully leverage the dashboard’s analytical power and developers to replicate or enhance similar features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Helpers Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the TopHelpersTable UI component. Explain its function in recognizing active contributors by counting user replies, fostering community engagement. Document the props interface with 'user' and 'cnt' fields representing helper names and their contribution counts. Describe the component's visual design consistency with other ranking tables and its grid placement using 'lg:col-span-1'. Explain the use of number formatting for reply counts and the conditional null state. Illustrate how this metric encourages positive participation and provide an example with sample contributor data. Address privacy considerations around user identification and suggest potential extensions like role-based filtering or streak tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Top Errors Ranking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the top errors ranking feature that detects and categorizes error messages in chat logs. Explain heuristic-based pattern matching used to identify common error formats (e.g., stack traces, HTTP codes, exception keywords). Detail how message content is scanned for error tokens, classified into types, and aggregated by frequency. Include implementation examples from the overview route showing conditional checks and category mapping. Discuss integration with the TopErrorsTable component and potential improvements like log parsing libraries or ML-based classification. Address noise reduction techniques and false positive mitigation.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the LLM integration system in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Explain how the application leverages OpenAI's Responses API to generate structured daily digests and insights from raw chat logs. Detail the process flow: from fetching messages in `generate/route.ts`, constructing prompts with context and instructions in `report.ts`, enforcing output format using Zod schema (`digest_schema.ts`), to validating and returning results. Discuss the importance of deterministic JSON output for downstream processing and UI rendering. Include examples of prompt engineering techniques used to guide model behavior. Address error recovery strategies when LLM output fails validation, timeout handling, and cost/performance trade-offs. Document how `smoke-openai.mjs` tests API connectivity and `smoke-digest.mjs` verifies schema compliance. This should empower developers to customize prompts, add new LLM features, or switch models confidently.

wiki_catalogs.name: Artifacts Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the ArtifactsTable UI component. Explain its role in tracking project artifacts and 'ship-it' moments, such as code snippets or deployment links. Document the component's handling of mixed artifact types through conditional rendering of URLs or 'code snippet' placeholders. Describe the props structure with 'id', 'url', 'hasCode', and 'preview' fields, and explain the absence of number formatting as it displays identifiers rather than metrics. Highlight the table's responsive overflow behavior and header labeling in Russian. Provide an example showing different artifact types. Discuss integration opportunities with CI/CD systems and suggest enhancements like artifact type icons or status badges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hashtags Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the hashtags analysis feature that extracts and ranks #tags used in messages. Explain how regex patterns detect hashtags in message text, normalize case, and aggregate usage counts. Detail the SQL or in-memory processing used to group hashtag occurrences and sort by popularity. Include examples from the API route showing extraction logic and how results are formatted for the HashtagsTable component. Discuss use cases such as trend monitoring and community topic tracking. Address challenges like multi-language hashtags, compound tags, and spammy tag inflation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Detail the primary API response structure defined in `schema.ts` and refined in `digest_schema.ts` using Zod. Define entities such as `ApiData` interface in `DashboardShell.tsx` representing KPIs, hourly/daily trends, top entities, and summary bullets. Explain field types, relationships, and constraints—for example, how `topUsers` maps user IDs to message counts and names. Document the `DailyDigest` type derived from Zod schema ensuring LLM outputs conform to expected shape. Include inferred database schema based on queries: `messages(message_id, chat_id, user_id, text, sent_at, raw_message)` and `users(id, first_name, last_name, username)`. Illustrate data validation rules, transformation pipelines, and serialization steps. Provide sample payloads from `/api/overview` and `/api/report/generate`. Address data lifecycle, caching potential, and performance implications of large result sets. This should help developers work with APIs and extend data structures reliably.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hashtags Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the HashtagsTable UI component. Explain its function in analyzing hashtag usage frequency, revealing topic trends and campaign effectiveness. Document the component's props with 'token' and 'cnt' fields for hashtag names and occurrence counts. Describe the consistent use of formatNumber for numerical values and the standard table styling pattern. Explain how this component helps identify emerging topics and provides insights into community interests. Provide a usage example with popular hashtags. Address challenges with hashtag normalization (case sensitivity, synonyms) and suggest improvements like topic clustering or popularity trend visualization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mentions Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the mentions analysis feature that tracks @user references across messages. Explain how regex parsing identifies mention patterns in message content and normalizes usernames. Detail the aggregation process that counts mentions per user and ranks them by frequency. Include code examples from the overview route showing extraction logic and integration with the MentionsTable component. Discuss applications in measuring influence or visibility within the chat community. Address edge cases like fake mentions, escaped characters, and cross-chat mention ambiguity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for environment configuration in tg-vibecoders-dashboard. List required variables: `DATABASE_URL` for PostgreSQL connection (Railway-compatible), `PORT` for server binding (default 3000), and optional `PGSSL=disable` to turn off SSL. Explain how `.env.example` serves as template for local setup and how values are loaded via `dotenv`. Show how `DATABASE_URL` is consumed in API routes like `overview/route.ts` during query execution. Discuss configuration best practices: never committing `.env`, using different URLs for staging/production, and validating connections before startup. Cover deployment-specific settings for platforms like Vercel or Render where environment variables must be set manually. Include troubleshooting tips for common issues like connection timeouts, SSL errors, or invalid credentials. This should enable smooth setup across environments and prevent configuration-related failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mentions Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MentionsTable UI component. Explain its purpose in tracking @user mentions to identify influential members and communication patterns. Document the props interface with 'token' for mention handles and 'cnt' for frequency counts. Describe the component's visual consistency with other ranking tables and its use of number formatting for counts. Explain how mention data can reveal collaboration networks and information flow within the community. Provide a practical example with sample mention data. Discuss privacy implications of mention tracking and suggest enhancements like mutual mention graphs or response time analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Developer Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create a practical developer guide for contributing to tg-vibecoders-dashboard. Start with setup instructions: Node.js 18+, copying `.env.example` to `.env`, installing dependencies with `npm i`, and running development server via `npm run dev`. Explain available scripts from `package.json`: `dev`, `build`, `start`, and testing commands like `smoke:openai` and `smoke:digest`. Demonstrate how to run smoke tests to verify OpenAI connectivity and schema validation integrity. Provide debugging tips—using `console.log` in API routes, inspecting network requests, and validating SQL queries. Include performance testing advice and suggestions for extending features like adding new tables or charts. This should lower the barrier to entry for new contributors and ensure consistent development practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Forwarded From Table
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the ForwardedFromTable UI component. Explain its unique role in identifying external channels that contribute content to the analyzed chat, measuring external influence. Document the complex rendering logic for channel identification, supporting URL links, titles, usernames, and raw chat IDs. Describe the props structure with 'url', 'title', 'username', 'chat_id', and 'cnt' fields. Explain the special empty state messaging in Russian when no forwards exist. Highlight the component's wide layout with 'lg:col-span-3' and its importance in understanding content sourcing. Provide an example with mixed channel types. Discuss data accuracy challenges with forwarded message attribution and suggest improvements like source verification or forward velocity metrics.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>
# Comprehensive Project Analysis: Telegram Dashboard

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The Telegram Dashboard project is an ultra-minimal viable product (MVP) designed to provide real-time analytics for Telegram chat activity over the last 24 hours or 7 days. It reads directly from an existing Railway Postgres database without requiring migrations, delivering insights through a simple Express server and static frontend.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Provide immediate visibility into Telegram message metrics
- Generate time-windowed analytics (24-hour and 7-day views)
- Deliver KPIs such as message volume, user engagement, and content trends
- Visualize top users, links, words, threads, and unanswered questions
- Integrate with OpenAI for structured digest generation

### Target Audience
The dashboard targets team leads, community managers, and developers who need quick insights into Telegram group activity patterns, engagement levels, and emerging topics without complex setup requirements.

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The application follows a Next.js architecture with API routes handling data processing and React components rendering visualizations. The backend connects directly to PostgreSQL, while the frontend uses Chart.js for data visualization.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[API Routes /api/overview]
    C --> D[PostgreSQL Database]
    C --> E[OpenAI LLM Integration]
    D --> C
    E --> C
    C --> B
    B --> F[React Components]
    F --> A
```

### Design Patterns
The system implements several key patterns:
- **Server-side data fetching**: All analytics are computed on-demand in API routes
- **Zod-based schema validation**: Ensures type safety for LLM responses and API outputs
- **Component composition**: DashboardShell orchestrates multiple specialized components
- **Environment-driven configuration**: Runtime behavior controlled by environment variables

### System Relationships
The architecture maintains clear separation between data access, business logic, and presentation layers. API routes serve as the integration point between database queries and frontend consumption.

### Data Flow Diagram
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    UI["Browser Client"] -->|HTTP Request| API["Next.js API Route"]
    API -->|Query| DB[(PostgreSQL)]
    DB -->|Message Data| API
    API -->|Process & Enrich| DATA["Processed Analytics"]
    DATA -->|Render Props| COMPONENTS["UI Components"]
    COMPONENTS -->|Visualize| UI
    API -->|LLM Payload| LLM["OpenAI Responses API"]
    LLM -->|Structured JSON| API
    API -->|Validate & Format| MD["Markdown Digest"]
    MD --> UI
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- `app/page.tsx`: Homepage component for 24-hour view
- `app/week/page.tsx`: Alternative page for 7-day view
- `app/api/overview/route.ts`: Primary API endpoint for analytics

Sources:
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [route.ts](app/api/overview/route.ts)

### Core Modules
- `lib/report/`: Contains digest schema definitions and rendering logic
- `lib/llm/`: Handles OpenAI integration and prompt engineering
- `app/components/`: Reusable UI components organized by category

Sources:
- [digest_schema.ts](lib/report/digest_schema.ts)
- [report.ts](lib/llm/report.ts)
- [DashboardShell.tsx](app/components/DashboardShell.tsx)

### Configuration Approach
Environment variables control critical aspects of the application including database connectivity, OpenAI integration, and performance tuning.

Sources:
- [package.json](package.json)
- [.env.example](env.example)

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies include:
- **Next.js 15.4.6**: React framework for server-side rendering
- **pg 8.12.0**: PostgreSQL client for Node.js
- **openai 5.12.2**: Official OpenAI SDK
- **zod 3.23.8**: Schema validation library
- **chart.js 4.5.0**: Data visualization library

Sources:
- [package.json](package.json)

### Integration Points
The system integrates with external services through:
- PostgreSQL database via connection string
- OpenAI API for structured content generation
- Environment variables for deployment configuration

Sources:
- [route.ts](app/api/overview/route.ts#L1-L523)
- [report.ts](lib/llm/report.ts)

### Component Relationship Diagram
```mermaid
graph LR
    DashboardShell --> KpiRow
    DashboardShell --> SummaryList
    DashboardShell --> HourlyChart
    DashboardShell --> DailyChart
    DashboardShell --> UnansweredTable
    DashboardShell --> TopErrorsTable
    DashboardShell --> TopHelpersTable
    DashboardShell --> TopThreadsTable
    DashboardShell --> TopLinksTable
    DashboardShell --> TopWordsTable
    DashboardShell --> ArtifactsTable
    DashboardShell --> HashtagsTable
    DashboardShell --> MentionsTable
    DashboardShell --> ForwardedFromTable
    DashboardShell --> ChatSelect
    DashboardShell --> WindowSelect
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The dashboard delivers comprehensive Telegram analytics including:
- Message volume and user engagement KPIs
- Temporal analysis (hourly/daily trends)
- Top contributors and popular content
- Thread analysis and unanswered questions
- Error pattern detection
- Forwarded content tracking

### Implementation Highlights
The `/api/overview` route performs sophisticated data processing including:
- Message text analysis for links, words, and hashtags
- Thread identification through reply chains
- Question detection using linguistic heuristics
- Helper leaderboard calculation via recursive SQL
- Forwarded content attribution

Sources:
- [route.ts](app/api/overview/route.ts)

### Feature Architecture Diagram
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> DataFetching: User loads page
    DataFetching --> Processing: Fetch from PostgreSQL
    Processing --> LLMProcessing: Prepare payload
    LLMProcessing --> LLMCall: OpenAI API request
    LLMCall --> Validation: Validate JSON response
    Validation --> Rendering: Render components
    Rendering --> Display: Show dashboard
    Validation --> Error: Invalid schema
    Error --> DisplayError: Show error state
    Display --> Idle: Complete
    DisplayError --> Idle: Complete
```
</blog>

wiki_readme.content:
# Telegram Dashboard — последние 24 часа / 7 дней

Ультра‑MVP дэшборд для чтения из существующей Railway Postgres без миграций и лишних фич. Один сервер (Express + pg) и одна статика.

## Запуск локально

1) Node 18+
2) Скопируй `env.example` → `.env` и заполни `DATABASE_URL`.
3) Установи зависимости и стартуй:

```bash
npm i
npm start
```

Открой:
- 24 часа: http://localhost:3000/
- 7 дней: http://localhost:3000/week.html

## Переменные окружения

- DATABASE_URL — строка подключения Railway (postgresql://...)
- PORT — порт (по умолчанию 3000)
- PGSSL — если SSL не нужен, установи `PGSSL=disable`

Пример см. в `env.example`.

## API

- GET `/api/overview` — последние 24 часа
- GET `/api/overview?days=7` — окно в днях (1..30)

Ответ содержит:
- `kpi` — total, unique, avg_per_user, replies, with_links
- `hourly` — почасовые группы
- `daily` — дневные группы (при `days>1`)
- `topUsers`, `topLinks`, `topWords`, `topThreads`
- `since`, `until`, `window_days`, `summaryBullets`

Все запросы считают окно времени на сервере и фильтруют по `sent_at`.

## Стек

- Node.js + Express + pg
- Один процесс, без Docker/миграций/аутентификации/логов и т. п.
- UI: простая статика + Chart.js через CDN

## Деплой (пример Railway/Vercel/Render)

- Нужен Node 18+, запусти команду `npm start`
- Прокинь `DATABASE_URL`, при необходимости `PGSSL=disable`

## Примечания

- Имя пользователя выводится как `Имя Фамилия (@username)`, если доступно.
- Из БД используются поля таблиц:
  - `messages(message_id, chat_id, user_id, text, sent_at, raw_message)`
  - `users(id, first_name, last_name, username)`
- Replies считаются по ключу `raw_message -> reply_to_message`.

## Диаграмма потока данных (простой дайджест)

```mermaid
graph TD
  UI["Клиент (браузер)"] -->|"Запрашивает дайджест за дату/чат"| API["Бэкенд (генерация дайджеста)"]
  API -->|"Запрашивает сообщения за сутки"| DB["Postgres (messages)"]
  DB -->|"Возвращает список сообщений"| API
  API -->|"Передаёт сообщения и строгую схему, чтобы получить структурированный дайджест"| LLM["OpenAI (Responses API)"]
  LLM -->|"Возвращает JSON дайджеста по схеме"| API
  API -->|"Проверяет JSON и преобразует в Markdown для чтения"| FMT["Форматирование Markdown"]
  FMT -->|"Готовит финальный текст дайджеста"| API
  API -->|"Отдаёт результат пользователю (JSON + Markdown)"| UI
```

## Тест GPT‑5

Пайплайн реализован и проверен GPT‑5. Быстрые проверки:

- `npm run smoke:openai` — проверка Responses API и строгого JSON вывода
- `npm run smoke:digest` — проверка схемы DAILY_DIGEST_SCHEMA на минимальном примере
